ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pasta alle vongole; mapped to broader 'pasta dish' class (FOODON_00002473).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers pasta dishes broadly; no specific vongole pasta entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for vongole pasta or pasta alle vongole as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of vongole pasta is approximately 300 g, comprising cooked pasta with clams in white wine sauce, typically served as a main course.

nutrient_source.title: Vongole Pasta — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Vongole Pasta (cr_id F05060133, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central clam and pasta composition data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — pasta (white, boiled) GI ~47–50
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for vongole pasta as a composite dish. GI of 49 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: white pasta (spaghetti/linguine), which has a published GI of approximately 47–50 (low) per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (49 × 13.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3) / 100 = 19.2 g. The white wine sauce and clam protein content are expected to modestly lower the effective GI of the composite dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 318 mg per 100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 954 mg sodium, representing ~41% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced salt during preparation, as clams naturally contribute sodium and additional seasoning can push total intake above safe thresholds.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 13.1 g per 100 g with a low GI of ~49; a 300 g serving provides approximately 39 g total carbohydrate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes can include vongole pasta in a meal plan by limiting portion to 150–200 g, choosing whole-wheat pasta to lower GI and increase fibre, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables to moderate the total glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), olive oil as the primary fat source providing monounsaturated fatty acids, and clams supplying omega-3 fatty acids and lean protein.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Vongole pasta prepared with extra-virgin olive oil and clams is broadly compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, sodium at 318 mg per 100 g warrants portion control and reduced added salt to keep total sodium within cardiovascular guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory components (olive oil oleocanthal, clam omega-3s) offset by moderate sodium and potential nightshade sensitivity from chili flakes.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory fats in this dish but should reduce added salt, omit chili flakes if nightshade-sensitive, and avoid the white wine component as alcohol is pro-inflammatory and interacts with RA medications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vongole pasta, known in Italian as pasta alle vongole, is a celebrated classic of Neapolitan and broader southern Italian coastal cuisine, originating in the Campania region around Naples. The dish is prepared by first boiling spaghetti or linguine to a firm al dente texture, while fresh or canned clams (vongole) are steamed open in a wide pan with extra-virgin olive oil, thinly sliced garlic, dried chili flakes, and a generous splash of dry white wine. Once the clams open and release their briny, mineral-rich cooking liquor, the drained pasta is tossed directly into the pan and finished over high heat so the pasta absorbs the sauce. Flat-leaf parsley is scattered over the top just before serving. The result is a dish with a firm, slightly chewy pasta texture coated in a thin, glossy, intensely savoury sauce that carries the clean ocean flavour of the clams, the aromatic warmth of garlic, a gentle heat from chili, and the bright acidity of white wine. The overall taste profile is briny, herbaceous, and umami-forward, with no dairy or cream involved. Per 100 g, vongole pasta provides 88 kcal, 3.5 g protein, 2.4 g total fat (predominantly monounsaturated from olive oil, with saturated fat estimated at ~0.4 g), 13.1 g carbohydrate, 1.7 g dietary fibre, 1.0 g sugar, 318 mg sodium, 41 mg calcium, and 0.73 mg iron. The glycemic index is approximately 49 (low), driven by the pasta component; the clam protein and olive oil further moderate the glycaemic response. Clinically, the dish presents moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from clams and seasoning (a 300 g serving delivers ~954 mg sodium), moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to the refined pasta carbohydrate load (mitigated by low GI and protein content), and low concern for cardiovascular disease given the favourable olive oil and seafood fat profile. The dish is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance, shellfish allergy, or sulphite sensitivity, and is not halal-certified due to white wine content. It is dairy-free and suitable for pescatarian diets.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05060133, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for pasta and clams. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (white pasta, GI ~49). All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Saturated fat of 0.4 g per 100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (pasta + clams + olive oil proportions) as HPB does not report this field. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a prepared vongole pasta entry as of 2026. GI of 49 is an analogous estimate based on white pasta (spaghetti/linguine) from University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for vongole pasta as a composite dish exists in approved sources. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (49 × 13.1 × 3) / 100 = 19.2 g. This dish is not halal-certified due to white wine content. Allergens include gluten (pasta), molluscs (clams), and sulphites (white wine). The input health_suitability field listed rheumatoid_arthritis as 'suitable: false' despite describing anti-inflammatory properties; this has been preserved in additional_conditions with a corrected concern_level of moderate reflecting the mixed profile.
